data.description:
## Context
Split from the dso code-review calibration epic (sure-foyer-aroma). Cross-patchset finding memory is
L-effort infrastructure orthogonal to the calibration work and is deferred so the calibration epic stays
focused. rebar reviews each patchset fresh today; the Gerrit gate already requires -has:unresolved.

## Success Criteria
- Repeat-noise across patchsets is reduced without importing dso's heavy cycle machinery
  (arbiter, relation taxonomy, oscillation loop) unless measurement justifies it.
- Neutrality preserved: Pass 1 never sees prior findings/defenses; memory applies only AFTER find+verify.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Carryover labeling: reuse the kernel novelty axis to mark 'carried from patchset N' and skip re-coaching.
- [ ] Resolved-suppression: a human-resolved-as-disagree finding, fingerprinted (path:line:content), is
      not re-raised while the code region is unchanged.
- [ ] Decision made (with data) on whether any oscillation/convergence signal is warranted.
data.title: Cross-patchset code-review finding memory (deferred from sure-foyer-aroma)
